questions.text: Magnetic field strength is measured in:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Wbm-1: This option suggests that magnetic field strength is measured in Wbm-1. The unit "Wbm-1" represents webers per meter squared, which is incorrect.
Wbm-2: This option suggests that magnetic field strength is measured in Wbm-2. The unit "Wbm-2" represents Weber per meter square, which is correct.
Wbm2: This option suggests that magnetic field strength is measured in Wbm2. The unit "Wbm2" represents Weber squared per meter, which is incorrect.

Wb: This option suggests that magnetic field strength is measured in Wb (weber). However, magnetic field strength is not measured in webers. Weber is the unit of magnetic flux, not magnetic field strength.

questions.explanation: Wbm-2: This option suggests that magnetic field strength is measured in Wbm-2. The unit "Wbm-2" represents webers per meter square, which is correct.

questions.text: A solenoid is cut into two halves. Magnetic induction due to the same current in each half will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The magnetic induction due to a current-carrying solenoid depends on factors such as the number of turns per unit length, the current flowing through it, and the permeability of the material within the solenoid. When a solenoid is cut into two halves, the number of turns per unit length and the current flowing through each half remain the same. Therefore, the magnetic induction due to the same current in each half will be the same as the original solenoid.

The magnetic induction due to a current-carrying solenoid depends on factors such as the number of turns per unit length, the current flowing through it, and the permeability of the material within the solenoid. When a solenoid is cut into two halves, the number of turns per unit length and the current flowing through each half remain the same. Therefore, the magnetic induction due to the same current in each half will be the same as the original solenoid.

The magnetic induction due to a current-carrying solenoid depends on factors such as the number of turns per unit length, the current flowing through it, and the permeability of the material within the solenoid. When a solenoid is cut into two halves, the number of turns per unit length and the current flowing through each half remain the same. Therefore, the magnetic induction due to the same current in each half will be the same as the original solenoid.

The magnetic induction due to a current-carrying solenoid depends on factors such as the number of turns per unit length, the current flowing through it, and the permeability of the material within the solenoid. When a solenoid is cut into two halves, the number of turns per unit length and the current flowing through each half remain the same. Therefore, the magnetic induction due to the same current in each half will be the same as the original solenoid.

questions.explanation: Magnetic field due to a solenoid, B=μo n I, where n= l/N. So, if the length and number of turns become half for a solenoid, n remains the same, and thus magnetic field remains the same.

questions.text: A coil of 600 turns is threaded by a flux of 8x10-5 webers if this flux is reduced to 3x10-5 webers in 0.015 seconds. The average induced e.m.f. is:

questions.text: An electron and a proton enter a magnetic field with equal velocities which one of them experiences more force:
questions.subject: Physics

As both proton and electron have the same velocity they will experience the same value of force.
As both proton and electron have the same velocity they will experience the same value of force.
As both proton and electron have the same velocity they will experience the same value of force.

questions.explanation: F=q(V×B) F=qvBsin The formula infers that F∝v As both proton and electron have the same velocity they will experience the same value of force. As both have opposite charges, therefore the force being applied would be the same in magnitude but opposite in direction. questions.text: A conductor of length L is moved across a magnetic field. Due to this activity, a potential difference appears across the ends of the conductor. This type of potential difference is termed as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Self-Induction: Self inductance is the induction of a voltage in a current-carrying wire when the current in the wire itself is changing. In the case of self-inductance, the magnetic field created by a changing current in the circuit itself induces a voltage in the same circuit. Therefore, the voltage is self-induced

Mutual Induction :When two coils are brought in proximity with each other the magnetic field in one of the coils tend to link with the other. This further leads to the generation of voltage in the second coil. This property of a coil which affects or changes the current and voltage in a secondary coil is called mutual inductance.

Motional EMF : Motional emf is the emf induced by the movement of the conductor through the magnetic field.ORAn emf induced by motion relative to a magnetic field is called a motional emf. This is represented by the equation emf = LvB, where L is length of the object moving at speed v relative to the strength of the magnetic field B.

Electrostatic potential : The electric potential (also called the electric field potential, potential drop, the electrostatic potential) is defined as the amount of work energy needed to move a unit of electric charge from a reference point to the specific point in an electric field.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Motional EMF. When a conductor of length L moves across a magnetic field B, a potential difference appears across its ends, which is termed as motional EMF. This is induced by the movement of the conductor through the magnetic field. Self Induction and Mutual Induction involve induction of voltage in coils due to changing currents and proximity to other coils, respectively. Electrostatic potential relates to the work energy needed to move electric charge in an electric field.

questions.text: Magnetic flux is a scalar product of _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Magnetic flux (Φ) is the scalar product of magnetic field (B) and the area (A) through which the magnetic field passes. It is given by the formula: Φ = B * A. The magnetic flux is a measure of the total number of magnetic field lines passing through a surface.
Magnetic Flux is the dot / scalar product of magnetic flux density B, and cross-sectional area of coil A. Therefore, Φ= B. A. Hence Option B is correct.
Magnetic flux is not the scalar product of magnetic field (B) and current (I). Instead, the magnetic flux is related to the number of magnetic field lines that pass through a surface.

options.text: None of these options are correct
options.explanation: This option is not correct because option b) "B and A" is the correct one. Magnetic flux is the scalar product of magnetic field (B) and the area (A) through which the magnetic field lines pass.

questions.explanation: Magnetic Flux Φ is the dot / scalar product of magnetic flux density B, and cross-sectional area of coil A. Therefore, Φ = B.A. Hence Option B is correct.

questions.text: An AC generator converts:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. An AC generator does not convert mechanical energy into heat energy directly. While some heat may be generated due to friction in the generator's moving parts, the primary purpose of the generator is to convert mechanical energy into electrical energy through the process of electromagnetic induction, as explained in option B.

An AC (alternating current) generator, also known as an alternator, converts mechanical energy into electrical energy. This process involves rotating a coil of wire (the rotor) within a magnetic field, which induces an alternating current in the wire due to Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction. The mechanical energy required to rotate the rotor is typically provided by a turbine, engine, or other mechanical sources.Therefore, option B) Mechanical energy to electrical energy is the correct choice.

This option is incorrect. An AC generator does not convert electrical energy into mechanical energy. Instead, it operates in the opposite direction, converting mechanical energy (from sources such as turbines, engines, etc.) into electrical energy, as explained in option B.

This option is also incorrect. An AC generator does not convert heat energy directly into electrical energy. While some heat may be generated during the operation of the generator due to friction and losses in the system, the primary conversion process involves mechanical energy being converted into electrical energy through electromagnetic induction, as explained in option B.

questions.explanation: A generator can convert mechanical/kinetic energy to electrical energy. It can produce either AC or DC current. An AC generator creates an alternating current that periodically reverses direction. But in a DC generator, a direct current flows in one direction.

questions.text: When the coil is rotated in the magnetic field, the current produced is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Constant: If a coil is rotated in a magnetic field at a constant speed, a sinusoidal alternating current (AC) is induced in the coil. The magnitude of the current changes sinusoidally over time, but the frequency remains constant.

Decrease: As the coil is rotated in a magnetic field, the induced current can decrease if the speed of rotation decreases or if the magnetic field weakens. This decrease in the rate of change of magnetic flux will result in a decrease in the induced current.

Increase: As the coil is rotated in a magnetic field, the induced current can increase if the speed of rotation increases or if the magnetic field strength increases. This leads to a higher rate of change of magnetic flux, which induces a larger current.

Continuously changing: This option is correct. When a coil is rotated in a magnetic field, the induced current is continuously changing due to the constantly changing magnetic flux. The direction and magnitude of the induced current depend on the speed and direction of rotation.

questions.explanation: Direction of current changes as the coil is rotated.

questions.text: Eddy currents flow in:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Open loops: Eddy currents do not flow in open loops. Eddy currents are circular currents that circulate within conductive materials when subjected to changing magnetic fields. An open loop does not form a closed path for these circulating currents.

Closed loops: This option is correct. Eddy currents flow in closed loops within conductive materials. When a magnetic field changes in intensity or direction, these circulating currents are induced within the material. They can cause energy loss in transformers, motors, and other electrical devices.
Straight lines: Eddy currents do not flow in straight lines. They are circular currents that form closed loops within conductive materials.
Vertical loops: Eddy currents do not specifically flow in vertical loops. The direction of the loops depends on the orientation of the changing magnetic field and the conductive material.

questions.explanation: Eddy currents are loops of electrical current induced within conductors by a changing magnetic field in the conductor according to Faraday's law of induction. Eddy currents flow in closed loops within conductors, in planes perpendicular to the magnetic field.

questions.text: When a bar magnet is broken from the middle, the broken pieces will have:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: North pole only: When a bar magnet is broken in half, each piece will have both a north pole and a south pole. The magnetic field lines originate from the north pole and terminate at the south pole. Breaking the magnet doesn't remove its magnetic properties.
South pole only: Similar to option (a), when a bar magnet is broken in half, each piece will have both a north pole and a south pole. The magnetic properties are not removed by breaking the magnet.

Both south and north poles: This option is correct. When a bar magnet is broken from the middle, each broken piece will have both a south pole and a north pole. This is a fundamental property of magnets.
Magnetism will be removed: This option is not correct. Breaking a bar magnet does not remove its magnetism. Each broken piece will still retain its magnetic properties.

questions.explanation: Even if a magnet is broken in half, each half has one north pole and one south pole.

options.explanation: faradays law doesnot depends on max magnetic flux

options.text: Rate of change of magnetic flux
options.explanation: faradays law depends on rate of change of magnetic flux
faradays law doesnot depends on change in magnetic flux
faradays law doesnot depends on initial magnetic flux

questions.explanation: Faraday's law of induction (to distinguish it from his laws of electrolysis), states that the magnitude of the emf induced in a circuit is proportional to the rate of change of the magnetic flux that cuts across the circuit.

questions.text: A transformer is based on the theory of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Self-inductance is a property of an electrical conductor or coil that causes the conductor to oppose changes in the current flowing through it. However, a transformer is not based solely on self-inductance.

This is a fact to be memorized. A transformer is based on the theory of mutual inductance.Mutual inductance is the property of two coils being in close proximity to each other, such that a change in current in one coil induces a voltage in the other coil. This is the fundamental principle on which a transformer operates. It uses mutual inductance to transfer electrical energy from one coil (called the primary coil) to another coil (called the secondary coil) without direct electrical contact. Hence, Option B is correct.

Capacitive effect refers to the property of capacitors where electric charge is stored between two conductive plates separated by an insulating material. Transformers do not work based on capacitive effects.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because a transformer is primarily based on the principle of mutual inductance, not on self-inductance or capacitive effects. Therefore, the correct option is b) Mutual inductance.

questions.explanation: This is a fact to be memorized. A transformer is based on the theory of mutual inductance. Hence, Option B is correct.

This option implies that the magnetic field's polarity reverses within the flux area, which is not a definition of magnetic flux.
This option implies a static condition of the magnetic field, which contradicts the dynamic nature of magnetic flux where changes in strength can occur.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is changes strength because magnetic flux is defined as the product of the magnetic field strength and the area through which it passes, and it can vary based on the field's intensity. The other options are incorrect because:Changes direction: While magnetic fields can change direction, this does not pertain directly to the definition of flux.Changes polarity: Polarity refers to the orientation of the magnetic field, which is not the same as flux.No change occurs: This contradicts the nature of flux, which is dynamic and can change based on various factors.

questions.text: Henry is the unit of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The unit of self inductance is Henry (H). Self inductance refers to the property of an electrical conductor or coil to oppose any change in the current flowing through it by generating an induced electromotive force (EMF) in the same conductor.

The unit of mutual inductance is also Henry (H). Mutual inductance is the property of two or more coils or conductors to induce an EMF in each other when the current in one coil changes. It measures the extent to which a changing current in one coil induces a voltage in another nearby coil.

options.text: Both Options A and B are correct
options.explanation: Inductance, in general, has an SI unit "Henry" (H). Since self and mutual inductance are types of inductance, therefore both will have the same SI unit. Hence, option C is correct.
The unit of E.M.F is volt. Hence it is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Inductance, in general, has an SI unit “Henry” (H). Since self and mutual inductance are types of inductance, therefore both will have the same SI unit. Hence, option C is correct.

questions.text: Lamination of the transformer core is made of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is correct because silicon steel is used in transformer cores to reduce energy losses due to its low hysteresis loss and high permeability.
This is incorrect. Although soft iron has high permeability, it is not typically used in modern transformers due to higher hysteresis losses compared to silicon steel.
This is incorrect. Aluminium is used as a conductor in transformers but not as a core material due to its poor magnetic properties.
This is incorrect. Cast steel is not used for transformer cores because it has higher magnetic losses compared to silicon steel.

questions.explanation: Silicon steel is used for transformer core laminations because it has low hysteresis loss, which helps in reducing energy losses in the transformer. It also has high permeability, allowing it to efficiently channel magnetic fields. Although soft iron has high permeability, it causes higher hysteresis losses, making it less efficient than silicon steel. Aluminium is not suitable as a core material due to its poor magnetic properties, though it is used as a winding material. Cast steel is also unsuitable due to higher magnetic losses compared to silicon steel.

questions.text: If a wire having current 10A has a 3T magnetic field, what will be the magnetic field at a point from the wire at double the distance?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Any current-carrying wire is surrounded by the magnetic field which is: B = µ0I/2πr It can be seen from the above equation that the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the distance and directly proportional to the current. When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.

questions.explanation: Any current-carrying wire is surrounded by the magnetic field which is B = µ0I/2πr. It can be seen from the above equation that the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the distance and directly proportional to the current. When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will reduce by a factor of two, which is 1.5 T.

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic flux is Weber which is equivalent to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The unit NA/m is the unit of magnetic field strength, also known as magnetic field intensity or magnetic field intensity. It represents the SI unit for magnetic field strength and is measured in amperes per meter (A/m).

The unit NmA is not a recognized unit in the SI system. Newton-meter (Nm) is the SI unit of torque or moment of force, representing the product of a force (measured in newtons) and the perpendicular distance from the axis of rotation (measured in meters). Milliampere (mA) is the SI unit for electrical current, representing one thousandth of an ampere. Combining the two units, NmA, would not have a specific meaning or representation in a recognized SI unit.

The unit Nm^-1A^-1 represents the reciprocal of the unit for electrical resistance, which is ohm (Ω). Reciprocal of ohm: (Ω)^-1 = Ω^-1 = Nm^-1A^-1 Therefore, Nm^-1A^-1 is the unit of electrical conductance, which is the measure of how easily electric current can flow through a material. It is commonly denoted by the unit siemens (S) in the SI system.

The SI unit of magnetic flux is indeed the Weber (Wb). The Weber is defined as the amount of magnetic flux that passes through a surface of one square meter (1 m²) perpendicular to a magnetic field of one Tesla (1 T). In terms of base SI units, one Weber is equivalent to one Tesla meter squared (T·m²).

questions.explanation: The formula for the magnetic field density (B) is: B=F/IL {where F is the force, I is the current and L is the length of the conductor.} Substitute the SI units into the equation: B=N/Am The magnetic flux is BA therefore: B=N/Am x m2 =NmA-1

questions.text: If a stationary bar magnet is placed near a coil so that maximum lines of force passes through the coil, the galvanometer shows:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If a stationary bar magnet is placed near a coil in such a way that maximum magnetic field lines pass through the coil, the galvanometer will show no deflection, hence this option is incorrect.
If a stationary bar magnet is placed near a coil in such a way that maximum magnetic field lines pass through the coil, the galvanometer will show no deflection, hence this option is incorrect.

The galvanometer will show no current when a stationary bar magnet is placed near a coil so that the maximum lines of magnetic force pass through the coil.When the magnet is stationary and its magnetic field is not changing, there will be no induced electromotive force (EMF) and therefore no induced current in the coil. The galvanometer will show no current.For induced current to flow, there must be a changing magnetic field. If you were to move the magnet relative to the coil, such as by sliding it back and forth, or rotating it, the magnetic field passing through the coil would change, and this changing magnetic field would induce an EMF and a corresponding induced current in the coil. The magnitude of the induced current would depend on factors such as the rate of change of the magnetic field and the properties of the coil.
If a stationary bar magnet is placed near a coil in such a way that maximum magnetic field lines pass through the coil, the galvanometer will show no deflection. Hence, this is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The galvanometer will show no current when a stationary bar magnet is placed near a coil so that the maximum lines of magnetic force pass through the coil.When the magnet is stationary and its magnetic field is not changing, there will be no induced electromotive force (EMF) and therefore no induced current in the coil. The galvanometer will show no current.For induced current to flow, there must be a changing magnetic field. If you were to move the magnet relative to the coil, such as by sliding it back and forth, or rotating it, the magnetic field passing through the coil would change, and this changing magnetic field would induce an EMF and a corresponding induced current in the coil. The magnitude of the induced current would depend on factors such as the rate of change of the magnetic field and the properties of the coil.

questions.text: Magnetic force on a charged particle is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This formula seems to be a combination of the basic components of the magnetic force equation, but it's not the complete formula. The correct formula includes the sine of the angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field vector, represented as sin(θ). Therefore, while QvBl captures some aspects of the magnetic force equation, but it's not fully accurate without considering the angle θ.

This option implies that none of the provided options are correct. However, one of the options does represent the correct formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle. As explained earlier, the correct formula is QvBsin(θ), where Q is the charge of the particle, v is the velocity of the particle, B is the magnetic field strength, and θ is the angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field vector.

his formula represents the component of the magnetic force parallel to the magnetic field, given by cos(θ). However, the correct formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle includes the perpendicular component of the force, represented by sin(θ). Therefore, while QvBcos(θ) is a valid component of the magnetic force, it does not represent the complete formula.

This is the correct explanation. The magnetic force (F) exerted on a charged particle moving through a magnetic field (B) with velocity (v) is given by the formula:F=QvBsin(θ)This formula accounts for the perpendicular component of the velocity vector concerning the magnetic field, as indicated by sin(θ). Therefore, option D) QvBsin(θ) represents the correct formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle.

questions.explanation: When a charge moves through a magnetic field, a force is applied on it which is given by:F = QvB sinθQvBl is the motional EMF produced when a conductor moves through a static magnetic field.

questions.text: Lenz’s law in electromagnetic induction is the direct consequence of the principle of conservation of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Lenz’s law is used to determine the direction of the induced current by a change in magnetic field. This direction of the induced current always opposes the direction of the magnetic field which produced it.Complete step by step answer:Lenz’s law states that the direction of an induced current by a changing magnetic field is such that the magnetic field created by the induced current opposes that change responsible for its production. This means, a change in magnetic field (B1) produces an induced current and the induce current also generates some magnetic field (B2) which is different from the initial magnetic field (B1). Now, the generated magnetic field (B2) by the induced current opposes the initial changing magnetic field (B1). Consider a magnet and a coil, such that the magnet is approaching towards the coil. When the north pole of a magnet is moved towards a coil, the face of the coil near to the magnet behaves like a north pole and tries to repel the approaching North Pole. To do this, the coil generates an anticlockwise current in itself. Here, we can see that electrical energy is not generated just by moving the magnet towards the coil. There is an opposite for the change in flux. Hence we can say that Lenz’s law is a consequence of conservation of energy. Thus, the right answer is Option A; Lenz’s law is a consequence of law of conservation of energy.
Lenz’s law is a consequence of law of conservation of energy.
Lenz’s law is a consequence of law of conservation of energy.
Lenz’s law is a consequence of law of conservation of energy.

questions.explanation: Lenz’s law is used to determine the direction of the induced current by a change in magnetic field. This direction of the induced current always opposes the direction of the magnetic field which produced it.Complete step by step answer:Lenz’s law states that the direction of an induced current by a changing magnetic field is such that the magnetic field created by the induced current opposes that change responsible for its production. This means, a change in magnetic field (B1) produces an induced current and the induce current also generates some magnetic field (B2) which is different from the initial magnetic field (B1). Now, the generated magnetic field (B2) by the induced current opposes the initial changing magnetic field (B1). Consider a magnet and a coil, such that the magnet is approaching towards the coil. When the north pole of a magnet is moved towards a coil, the face of the coil near to the magnet behaves like a north pole and tries to repel the approaching North Pole. To do this, the coil generates an anticlockwise current in itself. Here, we can see that electrical energy is not generated just by moving the magnet towards the coil. There is an opposite for the change in flux. Hence we can say that Lenz’s law is a consequence of conservation of energy. Thus, the right answer is Option A; Lenz’s law is a consequence of law of conservation of energy.

questions.text: In transmission from grid station, power losses are minimized by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Decreasing current directly is not feasible due to the relationship between voltage and current; instead, transformers adjust voltage to indirectly manage current.
While decreasing resistance can reduce power losses, it is not a practical approach for long-distance transmission due to material and cost constraints.
Increasing resistance would reduce current but would increase power losses through heat, making it inefficient for transmission.
By increasing voltage, the current decreases, resulting in reduced power losses, as described by the formula P = I^2R. This is the most efficient method for power transmission.

questions.explanation: Efficient power transmission is achieved by using transformers to increase the voltage, which reduces the current according to the formula P = IV, where P is power, I is current, and V is voltage. By decreasing the current, power losses due to resistance (P = I^2R) are minimized. Transformers allow the voltage to be stepped up for long-distance transmission, and then stepped down for safe distribution at the destination.Decreasing or increasing resistance directly is not practical or efficient due to cost and material limitations. Directly decreasing current is not feasible without a corresponding increase in voltage. Therefore, increasing the voltage is the correct and efficient method to minimize power losses.

questions.text: Face of coil having clockwise current:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the current flows in an anti-clockwise direction on the face of the coil, it behaves like a north pole according to the clock face rule. However, since the current is clockwise, this option is incorrect.
The correct answer. When current flows in a clockwise direction on the face of the coil, it behaves like a south pole, as per the clock face rule.

options.text: Becomes a magnet of varying poles
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the polarity of a coil does not vary with time; it is determined by the direction of the current in the coil.

options.text: Does not behave like a magnet
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because a current-carrying coil always behaves as a magnet, forming an electromagnet with defined north and south poles.

questions.explanation: A current-carrying loop or coil functions as an electromagnet. The direction of current flow determines the polarity of the magnetic field produced. Using the clock face rule: if you look at the face of the coil with current flowing in a clockwise direction, it behaves like a south pole. Conversely, if the current flows anti-clockwise, it behaves like a north pole. Therefore, the correct answer is that the face of the coil with a clockwise current behaves like the south pole.Option A is incorrect because clockwise current results in a south pole, not a north pole. Option C is incorrect because the polarity does not vary; it remains constant as long as the current direction is constant. Option D is incorrect because a current-carrying coil always behaves as a magnet, thus possessing magnetic properties with defined poles.

questions.text: If we make the magnetic field stronger, the value of induced current is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the magnetic field strength increases, the induced current cannot decrease.

The induced current in a conductor is given by Faraday’s Law of Electromagnetic Induction:E=-Nφ/tIf we increase the magnetic field strength , the rate of change of flux increases, leading to a greater induced EMF, and hence more induced current (assuming resistance remains constant).
The induced current does not vanish unless there is no change in flux or the circuit is open.
Since EMF depends on , increasing will change the induced current, so it cannot remain constant.

questions.explanation: Faraday's Law says that the emf induced (and therefore the current induced) in the loop is proportional to the rate of change in magnetic flux and therefore to magnetic flux.We know that magnetic flux = BAcos θWhere B is the magnetic flux density, A is the area and θ is the angle between a perpendicular vector to the area and the magnetic field.Thus, Induced emf (and therefore the induced current) is also proportional to magnetic field strength/magnetic flux density.

questions.text: Magnetic permeability has units as
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The SI unit of magnetic permeability is henries per meter (H/m) or newtons per ampere squared (N⋅A^−2).

questions.text: Emf = -N(ΔΦ/Δt) is according to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Faraday's Law: This law states that an electromotive force (emf) is induced in a closed loop of wire when there is a change in the magnetic flux through the loop over time. The equation "emf = -N(ΔΦ/Δt)" represents this law, where "emf" is the induced electromotive force, "N" is the number of turns in the loop, and "ΔΦ/Δt" is the rate of change of magnetic flux.Lenz's Law: This law is a consequence of Faraday's Law and provides the direction of the induced current in the loop. It states that the direction of the induced current is such that it opposes the change in magnetic flux that caused it. In other words, the induced current creates a magnetic field that counteracts the change in the external magnetic field, trying to maintain the status quo.both Faraday's Law and Lenz's Law are represented by the equation "emf = -N(ΔΦ/Δt)," as it shows the induction of emf due to the change in magnetic flux and the relationship between the induced current and the change in magnetic field.

questions.text: If a charge of 2C is moving with 5m/s enter at 30° in 3 T, calculate the force experienced by it.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: To calculate the force experienced by a charge moving through a magnetic field, you can use the formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle:F = q * v * B * sin(theta)Where:F = Force experienced by the charge (in Newtons)q = Charge of the particle (in Coulombs)v = Velocity of the particle (in meters per second)B = Magnetic field strength (in Tesla)theta = Angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field vector (in degrees)Given:q = 2 C (Coulombs)v = 5 m/s (meters per second)B = 3 T (Tesla)theta = 30°First, we need to convert the angle from degrees to radians:theta_rad = 30° * (π / 180°) ≈ 0.5236 radiansNow, we can calculate the force:F = 2 C * 5 m/s * 3 T * sin(0.5236 radians)F ≈ 2 * 5 * 3 * 0.5F ≈ 15 NThe force experienced by the charge is approximately 15 Newtons.

questions.text: The flux is the region where magnetic field:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Magnetic flux does not specifically depend on changes in the direction of the magnetic field, but rather on how many field lines pass through a surface.
This option is incorrect. While changes in field strength can affect the flux, flux itself is not defined by changes in strength, but by the number of field lines through a surface.
This is the incorrect option. Magnetic flux is not concerned with polarity changes; it involves the total number of magnetic field lines through a surface.
This option is correct because magnetic flux itself is a measure of the number of magnetic field lines through a surface area, irrespective of changes in the field.

questions.explanation: Magnetic flux is defined as the number of magnetic field lines passing through a given surface area. It is a measure of the magnetic field's presence and intensity in a specific region. The correct answer is that the flux is the region where the magnetic field does not change because the definition of magnetic flux is static; it does not imply a change in direction, strength, or polarity. Option D is correct because it aligns with the fundamental definition of magnetic flux. The other options incorrectly associate flux with changes in direction, strength, or polarity, which do not define the static nature of magnetic flux.

questions.text: F = e(v x B) is valid for:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electrons are negatively charged particles, and the Lorentz force equation applies to them. The direction of the force is opposite to that of protons due to their negative charge.
Protons are positively charged particles, and the Lorentz force equation applies to them. However, it is not exclusive to protons.
Neutrons are neutral particles with no charge, so the Lorentz force equation does not apply to them.
The Lorentz force equation, F = e(v x B), applies to any charged particle moving in a magnetic field, regardless of charge type (positive or negative).

questions.explanation: The Lorentz force equation, F = e(v x B), is applicable to any charged particle because it describes the force experienced by a charged particle moving through a magnetic field. This includes positive charges like protons and negative charges like electrons. The direction of the force depends on the particle's charge and the magnetic field direction. Neutrons, being neutral, do not experience a force in a magnetic field, which is why they are not applicable in this equation.

options.text: Tennis gives you plenty of exercise, it develops quickness of eyes and calls your brain, your thinking power into action.

options.text: Tennis gives you plenti off exercise, it develops quickness of eyes and limb your brain, your thinking power into action.
options.explanation: In this option, the spelling of 'plenty' is wrong, and the phrasal verb 'plenty off' does not exist as well. Moreover, a comma should not be placed after 'exercise', and should be replaced by a full stop, or a semicolon. As this option contains errors, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Tennis gives you plenti off exercise, it develops quickness of eyes and limb upon your brane, your thinking power over action.
options.explanation: In this option, the spelling of 'plenty' and 'brain' is incorrect. The phrasal verb 'plenty off' is also incorrect. The placement of a comma after 'exercise' is also wrong. As this option contains errors, it is incorrect.

options.text: Tennis gives you plenty of exercise; it develops quickness of eyes and calls your brain, your thinking power into action.
options.explanation: This option is grammatically correct as it uses a semicolon appropriately to connect two independent clauses. There are no spelling errors, making this the correct choice.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct because it uses proper punctuation and spelling throughout the sentence. The semicolon correctly links two related independent clauses. In contrast, Options A, B, and C contain various grammatical errors, including comma splices and spelling mistakes, which make them incorrect. Therefore, careful attention to punctuation and spelling is essential in identifying the correct answer.

questions.text: Statement and Argument Statement: Should admission to the professional degrees in Pakistan be given only on merit without any concession to any particular group of students? Arguments:(I) Yes. This will improve the quality of the professional institutes and degrees as they will be able to complete the degree successfully. (II) No. This will keep a large number of socially and economically backward students out of the reach of professional institutes and degrees.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Argument II is also very strong.

options.text: If only argument II is strong
options.explanation: Argument I is also strong.

options.text: If both I and II are strong.
options.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.

options.text: If neither I nor II is strong
options.explanation: Both arguments are very strong.

questions.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.

questions.text: A police officer stationed along the route of a parade has been ordered not to allow cars to cross the route while the parade is in progress. An ambulance driver on an emergency run attempts to drive an ambulance across the route while the parade is passing. Under these circumstances, the officer should:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Ask the driver to wait while the officer calls headquarters and obtain a decision.
options.explanation: Without any knowledge of police rules, common sense dictates that saving lives is the number one priority. An ambulance on an emergency run is on a mission to save a life. Lifesaving takes precedence over the desire for an uninterrupted parade, despite the officer's prior orders.

Lifesaving takes precedence over the desire for an uninterrupted parade, despite the officer's prior orders.

options.text: Hold up the ambulance in accordance with the order.
